error_signature: no entry in view if fields are missing
title: No entry in Supabase view when fields are missing
symptom: Creating a new entry with empty fields results in no data being displayed in the view. The view does not show any records until all fields are populated.
applies_when: Using Supabase to create a new entry
Some fields in the entry are left empty
not_this_if: All fields are populated when creating the entry
The view is not being queried after the entry creation
root_cause: The issue arises because the SQL view is using INNER JOINs, which require all joined records to exist. If any related record is missing due to empty fields, the entire row is excluded from the result set.
fix_steps: Modify the SQL view to use LEFT JOINs instead of INNER JOINs for all joins.
Update the view definition in Supabase with the new SQL query.
Test creating a new entry with empty fields to ensure it now appears in the view.
agent_instruction: Change the joins in the Supabase view to LEFT JOINs to allow for missing fields.
